content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


October 1, 1986


William L. Gates
Catalano & Gates
Market Post Tower Building
55 South Market Street, Suite 250
San Jose, CA 95113

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-86-282

Dear Mr. Gates:

This is to confirm our telephone conversation of
September 26, 1986 regarding your request for advice regarding
the duties of Santa Clara Supervisor Zoe Lofgren under the
Political Reform Act.1/ As I indicated, Commission Regulation
Section 18329(b)(1) (copy enclosed) provides that formal
written advice will be provided only to the person whose duties
are in question or that person's authorized representative. Accordingly, since you are not Mrs. Lofgren's
authorized representative, we are unable to provide the advice
you have requested.

If you would like to discuss this, please let me know.

Sincerely,

John G. McLean
Counsel
Legal Division

JGM:sm
Enclosure

_______________________
1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative
Code Sections 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are
to Title 1, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

LAW OFFICES

CATALANO & GATES
ROBERT R. CATALANO
DIANE H. NADLER

A PROFESSIONAL ASSOCIATION
MARKET POST TOWER BUILDING
55 SOUTH MARKET STREET, SUITE 250
SAN JOSE, CALIFORNIA 95113

WILLIAM L. GATES

(408) 288-8100

September 24, 1986

John Mc Lean, Esq.
Fair Politicial Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Mc Lean:

Pursuant to your request of September 23, 1986, I hereby
summarize my conversation with you and the conversation you had the
same date with Mr. Bob Weers of the County Counsel's office,
County of Santa Clara.

On the above date both of us indicated to you an urgent need
for an opinion relating to the vote of Mrs. Zoe lofgren, a member
of the Board of Supervisors of Santa Clara County. The urgency
continues to exist and the issue will arise again at 10:00 a.m. on
Tuesday, September 30, 1986.

By way of background, my client, Mr. Al Rodriguez retained
Mr. Manuel Sandoval to assist him with mitigation necessitated by
the construction of a power substation by P.G. and E in Morgan
Hill, California. This substation would effectively put my
client's radio station (KAZA), located on an adjacent site, out of
business. Mr. Sandoval was paid a fee for his services in May or
June, 1985 and my client is now requesting that the Board of
Supervisors approve a lease for his towers on surplus park land.

Mr. Sandoval's fees were paid in full by Mr. Rodriguez months
ago and he will have no financial gain as the result of any
decision made by the Board of Supervisors in this matter. I
attach his statement, made under penalty of perjury, for your
review.

Apparently, a legal dispute arose between Mr. Sandoval and
Supervisor lofgren's husband (John Collins) relating to an
alleged unpaid debt owed by Mr. Sandoval. Mr. Collins has filed a
law suit against Mr. Sandoval.

This issue was presented to you by Mr. Weers and myself and
you advised both of us that if Mr. Sandoval will receive no
economic benefit as a result of Mrs. Lofgren's vote on this
matter, then no conflict of interest exists relating to Mrs.
Lofgren's vote on the lease of park land presently being
considered by the Board.

John Mc Lean, Esq.
September 24, 1986
Page 2

     I request that you send written confirmation that, based upon
the above facts, your oral opinion to Mr. Weers and myself was as
stated in this letter.

     Thank you for your assistance.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        WILLIAM L. GATES

WLG:td
Enclosure

P.S.  I enclose a self-addressed, stamped envelope for your use.

DECLARATION OF MANUEL J. SANDOVAL
UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY

I, MANUEL J. SANDOVAL, declare as follows:

1.     During May and June of 1985, I was retained by MR.
ALBERT RODRIQUEZ on behalf of Radio Station KAZA to assist with
mitigation due to relocation of my client's transmission towers
and to assist in identifying a suitable site for purposes of said
relocation.

2.     The total fee for the above services was paid in 1985.
Since that time, only one additional payment was made for work
approximately one year after the initial payments.

3.     I will not receive any additional compensation if MR.
RODRIQUEZ is successful in obtaining a lease on County park
lands. My fees have been paid in full several months ago.

4.     I have no ownership interest in KAZA or any other
businesses owned by MR. RODRIQUEZ and will not benefit in any way
whatsoever if he is granted a lease by Santa Clara County.

5.     I have no intention of appearing before the Board of
Supervisors on this issue and while I may have a dispute with
Mrs. Lofgren's husband, I do not believe that fact should prejudice any decision involving my client. I will have no financial
gain or loss as a result of any decision made by the Board of
Supervisors in this matter.

     I declare under penalty of perjury under the laws of the
State of California that the foregoing is true and correct, and
that this declaration was executed on September 24, 1986 at San
Jose, California.

                                        _______________________
                                        MANUEL J. SANDOVAL

ACKNOWLEDGEMENT


COUNTY OF SANTA CLARA    )
                         ) ss
STATE OF CALIFORNIA      )

     On this 24th day of September, 1986, before me, the
undersigned Notary Public, personally appeared Manuel A. Santosky
______________, personally known to me to be the person whose name
is subscribed to the within instrument, and acknowledged that
he executed the same for the purposes therein contained.

     IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and official
seal on the date hereinabove written.


     ┌─────────────────────────────┐
     │ OFFICIAL SEAL               │              [signature]
     │ CHRISTINA ANN DE LUCA       │         NOTARY PUBLIC
     │ Notary Public-California    │
     │ SANTA CLARA COUNTY          │
     │                             │
     │ My Comm. Exp. Dec. 15, 1989 │
     └─────────────────────────────┘


My commission expires: December 15, 1989

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 1, 1986


William L. Gates
Catalano & Gates
Market Post Tower Building
55 South Market Street, Suite 250
San Jose, CA  95113

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-86-282

Dear Mr. Gates:

     This is to confirm our telephone conversation of
September 26, 1986 regarding your request for advice regarding
the duties of Santa Clara Supervisor Zoe Lofgren under the
Political Reform Act.1/  As I indicated, Commission Regulation
Section 18329(b)(1) (copy enclosed) provides that formal
written advice will be provided only to the person whose duties
under the Act are in question or that person's authorized
representative.  Accordingly, since you are not Mrs. Lofgren's
authorized representative, we are unable to provide the advice
you have requested.

     If you would like to discuss this, please let me know.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        John G. McLean
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

Enclosure


_________________
1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative
Code Sections 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are
to Title 1, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code.


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: Commission Regulation Section 18329(b)(1) provides that formal written advice will be provided only to the person whose duties under the Act are in question or that person's authorized representative. Accordingly, since you are not Mrs. Lofgren's authorized representative, we are unable to provide the advice you have requested.
sections.question_synthetic: Does Santa Clara Supervisor Zoe Lofgren have a conflict of interest regarding her vote on a lease matter when her husband has a legal dispute with a party whose consultant was previously paid by the applicant?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the requester (William L. Gates) was not an authorized representative of Supervisor Lofgren, and Commission regulations limit formal written advice to the person whose duties are in question or their authorized representative.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes incoming request letter from William L. Gates (September 24, 1986) requesting written confirmation of prior oral advice, FPPC declination letter (October 1, 1986), and supporting declaration by Manuel J. Sandoval. The FPPC's response is a declination rather than substantive advice letter. The oral advice referenced suggested no conflict exists if the interested party receives no economic benefit from the supervisor's vote.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Santa Clara Supervisor Zoe Lofgren have a conflict of interest regarding her vote on a lease matter when her husband has a legal dispute with a party whose consultant was previously paid by the applicant?

CONCLUSION: Commission Regulation Section 18329(b)(1) provides that formal written advice will be provided only to the person whose duties under the Act are in question or that person's authorized representative. Accordingly, since you are not Mrs. Lofgren's authorized representative, we are unable to provide the advice you have requested.

embedding.summary: This file contains a request for FPPC advice regarding potential conflict of interest issues for Santa Clara Supervisor Zoe Lofgren, along with the FPPC's declination to provide written advice due to the requester lacking proper authorization.
